content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 26, 1993

Judge Morton R. Colvin
318 Whitethorn Court
Windsor, CA 95492

Re: Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No. I-93-131

Dear Judge Colvin:

You have requested advice concerning your reporting
obligations under the conflict of interest disclosure provisions
of the Political Reform Act.1/ Since your letter concerns past
reporting obligations, we consider it to be a request for informal
assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).2/

QUESTION

As a retired judge, are you required to file statements of
economic interests (Form 721)?

CONCLUSION

Retired judges who serve or expect to serve 30 days or more
in any 12-month period must file statements of economic interests.

FACTS

You retired as a judge in February 1990. Subsequently, you
have served as a retired judge pro tem.

_________________________

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

Judge Morton Colvin
Page Two

ANALYSIS

Regulation 18724 (copy enclosed) states that court
commissioners and pro tem or retired judges are not required to
file statements of economic interests pursuant to Government Code
Section 87200, et seq., if they serve or expect to serve less than
30 days in any 12-month period.

Once you expect to serve or you have served 30 days or more
in any 12-month period, you must begin filing statements of
economic interests with the clerk of the court to which you have
been assigned. Since there appears to be confusion regarding your
filing obligations as a retired judge, you may commence filing
your 1992 annual statement. You must continue filing annual
statements until your assignment ends. Once your assignment ends
and you do not receive a new assignment, you must file a leaving
office statement within 30 days of leaving your last assignment.

If you have any questions or need further assistance, please
contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Dixie Howard
Legal Documents Examiner
Technical Assistance Division

Enclosure
